# @lindaui design system

> React 19 design system wrapping **HeroUI v3** (react-aria-components, RAC) behind
> stable, per-component entry points, plus brand tokens in CSS. Published on npm under
> the `@lindaui` scope. This file is an index for coding agents — each package below has
> its own self-contained `llms.txt` with install, setup, conventions, and the full API
> catalog of every entry point.

This is HeroUI **v3** (RAC), NOT NextUI v2 — do not assume v2 "flat" props or names.
Three publishable packages, composed bottom-up: `@lindaui/tokens` (CSS) ← `@lindaui/ui`
(components) ← `@lindaui/blocks` (composed sections).

## Packages

- [@lindaui/ui](./packages/ui/llms.txt): 71 React component entry points over HeroUI v3
  — buttons, inputs, controls, select/combobox, dialogs, menu, table, charts primitive,
  hooks (`use-disclosure`, `use-media-query`) and utils (`search`, `cn`). Flat ergonomic
  APIs where it matters; compound re-exports where composition is too deep.
- [@lindaui/blocks](./packages/blocks/llms.txt): 22 composed sections built on
  `@lindaui/ui` — login-form, auth-layout, list-pane, detail-panel, confirm-dialog,
  split-workspace, media viewers, and 9 chart blocks. Generic, controlled, mobile-first.
- [@lindaui/tokens](./packages/tokens/llms.txt): the brand/design-token CSS bundle
  (HeroUI styles + OKLCH teal tokens + Tailwind v4 utilities). One import, no JS.

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm add @lindaui/ui @lindaui/blocks @lindaui/tokens
```

```tsx
import "@lindaui/tokens/css";              // once, at app root
import { Button } from "@lindaui/ui/button";
import { LoginForm } from "@lindaui/blocks/login-form";
```

Peers you provide: `react >=19`, `react-dom >=19`, `react-hook-form >=7`, and
`recharts >=3` for chart blocks. Import per entry point — there is no root barrel.

## Critical conventions (full detail in each package's llms.txt)

- Controls emit `onChange`, NOT `onValueChange`.
- `select`/`combobox`: flat `items` + `onChange(value)` — there is no `SelectItem`.
- `dialog`: controlled `open`/`onClose` — there is no `ModalContent`.
- `button`: `variant` (`primary|secondary|ghost|danger|link`), NOT `color`.
- Dark mode: toggle a `.dark` class on an ancestor.

## Custom theme (per project)

The whole system is driven by CSS custom properties read at runtime, so a consumer
re-skins everything by overriding the **canonical** vars after the base import — no
rebuild, no component changes:

```tsx
import "@lindaui/tokens/css";   // default (teal) theme
import "./theme.css";            // your brand — overrides --primary, --card, --ring, --radius, --chart-1..5, ...
```

Override ONLY the canonical vars (not the HeroUI `--accent`/`--surface`/... or the
`--color-*` utilities — they reference the canonical ones). A commented starting point
ships as `@lindaui/tokens/theme-template.css`. Runtime-switchable themes: scope each
under `[data-theme="x"]` instead of `:root`. Full detail in
[@lindaui/tokens](./packages/tokens/llms.txt).

## Source

Repo: https://github.com/francocarballar/lindaui — for the exact type of any export,
read its `.d.ts` in `node_modules/@lindaui/<pkg>/dist/<name>.d.ts`.
